published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation establishes the bounded-odds randomization framework, but not this particular union-tail functional; its super-uniformity is a routine composite-null randomization-p-value argument.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax — headline records exact equality at Gamma=1 for the two-sided doubled-tail practice.

published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation supplies the Gamma=1 uniform randomization model; the displayed equality is the elementary reversal-symmetry reduction.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax — headline records the verified two-sided strict gap 0.158 at Gamma=2.

published_prior_art.cite: FogartySmall2016MultipleComparisons concerns common assignment probabilities across multiple outcomes, not this single-statistic opposite-tail witness.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax explicitly banks the exact joint two-sided improvement region and implementable level-indexed procedure as the unproved follow-on gap.

published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum2018Stratified gives a conservative Taylor/separable calculation for heterogeneous strata; CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame gives an asymptotic multivariate game; Zhao2019SensitivityValue treats pair-matched sensitivity values. None gives an exact finite union-tail certificate and support-cell partition for one heterogeneous statistic.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 (thm:gamma-one-equality)
novelty_flags.one_line: The exact Gamma=1 equality is already recorded in the downgraded stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax headline; retain it only as inherited sanity/regression substrate.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition 1 (prop:gamma-two-gap-witness)
novelty_flags.one_line: The exact Gamma=2 gain 19/120 is already recorded in the downgraded stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax headline; it cannot be presented as new kernel content.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: Zhao2019SensitivityValue is a closer published comparator for the conventional pair-matched two-sided doubled-smaller-tail workflow and is absent from the checklist and related work.

novelty_flags.label: related_work discussion of CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says that this paper's discussion identifies weak-null sensitivity inference with hidden confounding as open, but the accessible preprint contains no weak-null discussion; Fogarty2023WeakNulls is the appropriate source.

novelty_flags.label: Rosenbaum2018Stratified comparator promise
novelty_flags.one_line: The promise table calls the proposed method a strict tightening of Rosenbaum2018Stratified, but no statement asserts an exact inequality or strict example against that paper's Taylor bound; downgrade it to informed-by or add the comparison.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 (oeq:operational-joint-certificate)
soundness_flags.one_line: Exact finite termination and a checkable certificate are undefined for arbitrary real-valued scores and Gamma without an input representation and computational model; restrict to rational/algebraic encodings or state an exact-real model and certificate format.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All inferential statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claims condition on Fisher's sharp null and the Rosenbaum conditional product-law model.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Validity and calibration concern conditional sharp-null randomization inference only, not weak-null or heterogeneous-effect inference.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1 (thm:gamma-one-equality)
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The no-hidden-bias equality additionally assumes a score-reversing assignment bijection.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The equality is asserted only for reversal-symmetric score designs with t_obs>0.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Proposition 1 (prop:gamma-two-gap-witness)
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The 19/120 calculation is one finite design-record witness.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: It establishes non-equivalence of the two calibrations, not a uniform lower bound on their gain.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Zhao2019SensitivityValue as the direct published doubled-tail comparator, quote its two-sided convention, and state precisely why its pair-matched asymptotics do not compute p_J.

Specify rational score and Gamma inputs, bit encoding, the exact certificate object, and whether pricing is exact or oracle-based; then state finite-termination and verification guarantees in those terms.

Either replace the Rosenbaum2018 strict-tightening promise with informed-by language or add a theorem/OEQ asserting p_J is bounded by its named Taylor calibration and strictly smaller on an explicit heterogeneous family.

Move the inherited Gamma=1 equality and 19/120 witness into an explicitly credited substrate/sanity section, leaving the exact certificate and strictness-cell characterization as the sole new field-level kernel.

recommended_next_step: Revise the computational specification and comparator map, credit the inherited repository fixtures, then re-run novelty review on the exact finite certificate kernel.
